title: Films & TV Shows

vocab.plot: the main events of a film or story
vocab.cast: all the actors in a film or play
vocab.series: a set of television or radio programmes with the same characters
vocab.episode: one part of a television or radio series
vocab.script: the written text of a film, play, or programme

phrases: On the one hand... on the other hand...

questions: What kind of films do you enjoy?
Do you prefer watching films at home or at the cinema?
How often do you watch TV?
Do you have a favourite TV series?
Have you ever watched a film in English?
answers: I would say I enjoy action films and comedies the most. I like films that are fast-paced but also have some humour.
I prefer watching films at home because it is cheaper and more comfortable. However, the cinema is better for big action films with special effects.
I do not watch much traditional TV anymore. I mostly watch series on streaming platforms, usually one episode a day.
My favourite series is a British crime drama. The plot is clever and the characters are very believable.
Yes, I often watch films in English with subtitles. It helps me improve my listening skills and learn natural expressions.

part2.question: Why do many people prefer watching TV series to films?
part2.photoA: A group of people watching a long film in a cinema
part2.photoB: A person watching multiple episodes of a series on a laptop
part2.model: In photo A, people are watching a film which tells a complete story in a few hours. In photo B, someone is binge-watching a series, which develops characters over many episodes. I think series are popular because viewers become attached to the characters and want to know what happens next. Films are good for a single experience, but series create long-term interest. However, watching too many episodes can be addictive and time-consuming.

part3.task: A school film club wants to attract more members. Discuss the ideas and decide which two would be most effective.
part3.model:
A: I think weekly screenings would be the foundation of the club because people need a regular event to attend.
B: That's true, but discussions after films would make it more engaging and help members share opinions.
A: I agree. A local filmmaker might be interesting, but it could be hard to organise.
B: Short film competitions could encourage creativity, but they might only appeal to people who want to make films.
A: So let's choose weekly screenings and post-film discussions.
B: Yes, I agree with you.

questions: Are subtitles helpful for learning English?
What makes a TV series successful?
answers: I think films can influence society by showing different perspectives. However, they usually reflect society more than they change it.
I believe children should watch less passive TV and more educational content. Parents should also set clear limits on screen time.
Yes, subtitles are very helpful because they connect spoken and written English. I started with subtitles and gradually stopped needing them.
A successful series needs good characters, an interesting plot, and enough mystery to keep people watching. Strong acting is also essential.

homework.reading: Read a film review online. Note the reviewer's opinion and the reasons given.
homework.listening: Watch an English-language film trailer. Write down five words or phrases used to describe it.
homework.writing: Write an essay (140–190 words): 'Is it better to watch films at home or at the cinema?'
